definitions.gloss_en: a competitive match or bout between two players or sides
definitions.gloss_jp: 二人または二陣営が競い合う試合・勝負
definitions.explanation_en: The general frame for a game session understood as a competitive encounter. Used across all Japanese game genres for head-to-head play.
definitions.implicit_notes: Implies adversarial structure; used even when the game has cooperative elements if sides are opposed.
definitions.orthography_notes: Always written in kanji (対戦). No katakana variant in standard usage. The compound uses 対 (facing / opposing) + 戦 (war / battle).
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal across board games, video games, and TCGs. In miniature wargames, frames the entire game session as a single match. JWC wargame glossary uses 対戦 as the default term for a game-length encounter.

usage_jp: {対|たい}{戦|せん}する (to play a match); {対|たい}{戦|せん}{相|あい}{手|て} (opponent); {対|たい}{戦|せん}{表|ひょう} (match record / pairing sheet)
usage_en: "match" in competitive contexts; "game" when referring to a single session.
comparative_notes: Preferred over 試合 (shiai) in board and miniature wargame contexts, where 試合 reads as sports-specific. 対局 (taikyoku) is the shogi/go equivalent but is not used outside those domains.

examples.japanese: {対|たい}{戦|せん}{相|あい}{手|て}を{甘|あま}く{見|み}るな。

cross_references.note: 戦場 — the battlefield on which the 対戦 takes place
